title: scabiosa cinerea subsp. cinerea
intro: Scabiosa cinerea subsp. cinerea is a subspecies of the Scabiosa cinerea plant, a type of flowering plant in the honeysuckle family (Caprifoliaceae) native to Europe. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, and its medicinal properties include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Scabiosa cinerea subsp. cinerea are said to include relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. Scabiosa cinerea subsp. cinerea can be prepared into various herbal remedies, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to harness its medicinal properties.
